title: Boat Rental in Los Roques 2026: Costs, Tips & Best Boats
html:
Setting off from Gran Roque, the main island of Los Roques, you can rent a motorboat for around 200 to 800 EUR per day. Whether you are after a skippered day trip or a bareboat adventure, BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators for a seamless booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Los Roques?

Daily rates range from 150 EUR for a shared tour to 1,500 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht. Prices vary by boat size, season, and whether you include fuel and crew. For a transparent overview of current listings, check the boat rental Los Roques page on BoatHire24.

  • Shared tour (around 4 hours): 150, 250 EUR per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): 400, 600 EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): 800, 1,200 EUR
  • Full crewed yacht (with skipper and host): 1,000, 1,500 EUR per day

Where to set off in Los Roques

The archipelago has several departure points, but most charters begin at Gran Roque harbour, where you will find the port captain's office and a line of pangas and speedboats. Other key spots include Cayo de Agua, a sandbar anchorage ideal for swimming, and Madrizquí, known for its clear waters and coral gardens. Further southeast, Crasquí and Noronquí offer quieter moorings. The distance from Gran Roque to Cayo de Agua is roughly 9 nautical miles, making it a popular one-hour run.

Which boat to rent in Los Roques

  1. Panga (open launch) Best for small groups exploring shallow flats; carries up to 4 guests; from 200 EUR half-day.
  2. Speedboat (e.g. Axopar 28) Ideal for day trips to distant cays; seats up to 8 guests; from 500 EUR half-day. See the Limpopo listing for a typical example.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon 40) Spacious for families or two couples; up to 12 guests; from 900 EUR full-day with skipper.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (Maiora 26m) For premium charters, onboard chef and tender included; hosts up to 12 guests; from 1,500 EUR per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Los Roques?

For powerboats under 10 HP, no licence is required; larger or faster boats must be operated by a licensed skipper. Venezuelan regulations generally follow international standards, but the operator will confirm exact requirements before booking. Always specify your qualifications when enquiring.

At Los Roques the trade winds blow strongest from December to March, so book a morning trip to avoid afternoon chop. Most experienced skippers will suggest anchoring at Cayo de Agua before the lunch rush.

At a glance

  • Price range: 150, 1,500 EUR per day
  • Peak season: December to March (northeast trade winds)
  • Typical trip duration: 4, 8 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on most yachts
  • Currency: EUR (prices often quoted in euros by operators)

Questions about boat rental in Los Roques

Can I rent a boat in Los Roques without a skipper?

Yes, for small outboard pangas up to 10 HP you can drive yourself, though you must show basic competence.

What is the best month for sailing in Los Roques?

April to June offer light winds and calmer seas, while the high season (December to March) brings steady trade winds and higher prices.

How far in advance should I book a boat in 2026?

For luxury crewed yachts, booking three to six months ahead is wise; smaller boats can often be reserved a few weeks before arrival.

Whether you are planning a family day out or a week-long expedition through the keys, BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare boats, read reviews, and book securely. Owners in the archipelago can also list your boat to reach thousands of travellers each month.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Los Roques and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
